channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel dies from cancer at age 78
item.description: Greg Gumbel, a longtime CBS sportscaster who broke barriers during his career calling some of the biggest sporting events, has died from cancer, according to a statement from his family released by the network on Friday. He was 78.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ed Greene, ‘a real trailblazer’ as Contra Costa basketball coach, dies at 83
item.description: Ed Greene represented Contra Costa College for parts of five decades as a player, coach and administrator
item.dc:creator: Evan Webeck, The Mercury News

item.title: Dodgers set to re-sign outfielder Teoscar Hernández for $66 million over 3 years, AP source says
item.description: Teoscar Hernández is headed back to the Los Angeles Dodgers. The free agent outfielder has agreed with the reigning World Series champions on a $66 million, three-year contract, a person familiar with the negotiations told The Associated Press late Friday night.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Corbin Burnes and Arizona Diamondbacks agree to $210 million, 6-year deal, AP source says
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Corbin Burnes and the Arizona Diamondbacks have agreed to a $210 million, six-year contract, a person familiar with the negotiations told The Associated Press late Friday night. The person spoke to the AP on condition of anonymity because the deal was pending a successful physical.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mets and pitcher Sean Manaea finalize $75 million, 3-year deal
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sean Manaea and the New York Mets finalized a $75 million, three-year contract Friday night that keeps one of the team's best pitchers last season at the front of a remodeled rotation. The sides agreed to terms earlier this week, subject to a successful physical.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syracuse's Kyle McCord breaks Deshaun Watson's ACC season passing yards record
item.description: Kyle McCord broke Deshaun Watson’s Atlantic Coast Conference season passing yards record on a 50-yard completion to Darrell Gill Jr. on No. 22 Syracuse’s first play of the second half in a 52-35 Holiday Bowl victory overt Washington State on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Taylen Green leads Arkansas to 39-26 victory over Texas Tech in the Liberty Bowl
item.description: Taylen Green threw for 341 yards and two touchdowns and ran for 81 yards in Arkansas’ 39-26 victory over Texas Tech on Friday night in the Liberty Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Clay Bailey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former Miami basketball coach Jim Larrañaga left 'exhausted' by NIL's impact on college sports
item.description: The longtime Hurricanes coach said his decision to abruptly step down from his position was partly due to his lack of interest in contending with NIL.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sources: Basketball star Jalen Stokes wins appeal, granted eligibility at Dougherty Valley
item.description: Dougherty Valley basketball player Jalen Stokes could make his debut on Friday night in Santa Barbara
item.dc:creator: Joseph Dycus, The Mercury News

item.title: NBA coaches react with dismay over firing of 2-time coach of the year Mike Brown
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — Not even two years ago, Rick Carlisle publicly lauded Mike Brown for the job he did on the way to winning the NBA's coach of the year award. And on Friday, Carlisle was among the coaches reacting with dismay that Brown was fired.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mavs star Luka Doncic will be out about a month before check on calf injury, AP source says
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Dallas Mavericks superstar Luka Doncic will be out about a month before being re-evaluated for a calf injury sustained in a Christmas Day loss to Minnesota, a person with knowledge of the injury said Friday.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles Dodgers agree to new deal with Teoscar Hernández: report
item.description: Los Angeles Dodgers fans will need to stock up on sunflower seeds after the team agreed to a new deal with fan favorite slugger Teoscar Hernández. Hernández will return to the Dodgers on a new 3-year, $66 million deal, according to ESPN's Jeff Passan.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel dead from cancer at 78
item.description: David Berson, president and CEO of CBS Sports, described Gumbel as breaking barriers and setting standards for others during his years as a voice for fans in sports, including in the NFL and March Madness.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Sportscasting icon Greg Gumbel dies at 78
item.description: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel, best known for his long career with CBS Sports, has died Friday, Dec. 27, according to CBS Sports and Gumbel's family.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Simone Biles sports custom Jonathan Owens outfit to Bears' final home game
item.description: Olympic gymnast Simone Biles wore a custom outfit dedicated to her husband, Chicago Bears safety Jonathan Owens, to his team's final home game of the season on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Longtime sports anchor, announcer Greg Gumbel dies at 78
item.description: Longtime announcer and anchor Greg Gumbel, who called NFL games and was a CBS studio host for college basketball, has died at the age of 78.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kings fire coach Mike Brown less than halfway through his 3rd season
item.description: The Sacramento Kings have fired coach Mike Brown less than halfway through his third season with the team mired in a five-game losing streak.
item.dc:creator: Josh Dubow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Eagles' Jalen Hurts still in concussion protocol, likely out vs Cowboys
item.description: Philadelphia Eagles head coach Nick Sirianni said it's going be "tough" for quarterback Jalen Hurts to play against the Dallas Cowboys, given where he is in the concussion protocol.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sarrazin to undergo surgery after head injury in crash in downhill training on 2026 Olympics slope
item.description: BORMIO, Italy (AP) — French standout Cyprien Sarrazin was in intensive care with a head injury and will undergo surgery after a crash during training for a World Cup downhill race on Friday on the slope set to be used for the 2026 Winter Olympics.
item.dc:creator: DANIELLA MATAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dodgers' Mookie Betts shares message to Yankees fans who tried to pry ball out of his glove in World Series
item.description: Los Angeles Dodgers right fielder Mookie Betts revealed his message for the the Yankees fans who tried to pry the ball out of his glove during Game 4 of the World Series.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former NCAA quarterback Tommy Lazzaro dies in 'tragic' accident at US Air Force base
item.description: Tommy Lazzaro, an Army sergeant and former quarterback for the Central Michigan Chippewas, has died following an accident at Eglin Air Force Base in Florida. He was 27.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, Fox 5

item.title: Meet the Americans who dominated the 2024 Paris Olympics
item.description: This past year was filled with champions galore as Team USA showed up in Paris for the 2024 Games prepared for total domination. Nearly 600 U.S. athletes competed to bring home 126 medals.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Beyoncé's performance highlighted Netflix's NFL debut on Christmas Day
item.description: Beyoncé provided more excitement than either game during Netflix’s NFL debut on Christmas Day. Riding into her halftime appearance on a white horse, the 32-time Grammy winner rocked her hometown Houston crowd with a nearly 13-minute performance on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Blackhawks drop 2nd straight on road trip in 4-3 loss to Wild
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Brock Faber snapped a tie in the third period, and the Minnesota Wild stopped a four-game losing streak by edging the Blackhawks, 4-3, on Monday night. Kirill Kaprizov, Jared Spurgeon and Marcus Foligno also scored for Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Jazz winning streak ends at Cleveland, 124-113
item.description: CLEVELAND (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Jazz first winning streak of the season did not last very long. Darius Garland scored 23 points and the NBA-best Cleveland Cavaliers beat the Utah Jazz 124-113 on Monday night for their 17th victory in 18 home games.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Houston Astros welcome 1B Christian Walker to team; say negotiations with Bregman stalled
item.description: The Astros welcomed first baseman Christian Walker to the team Monday, in one of two moves that almost certainly marks the end of Alex Bregman’s time in Houston.
item.dc:creator: KRISTIE RIEKEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: O'Connell passes for 257 yards and Raiders snap 10-game skid with 19-14 win over Jaguars
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Aidan O'Connell returned from an injury and passed for 257 yards, and the Las Vegas Raiders ended a 10-game losing streak with a 19-14 victory over the Jacksonville Jaguars on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Bucs' Mayfield loses key member of offense before his Texas homecoming
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers have another difficult road test in their attempt to win the NFC South title.
item.dc:creator: Dan Lucas, WFLA, NBC

item.title: John Seidler takes over as control person of Padres after Peter's death
item.description: John Seidler, an entrepreneur and business executive, has been named the next control person of the San Diego Padres, pending approval by Major League Baseball, after the death of his brother Peter Seidler, the team's former owner.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: Lamar Jackson and the Ravens draw even atop the AFC North, beating the 1st-place Steelers 34-17
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Lamar Jackson was solid in Baltimore's biggest game of the season so far. Then, after one of the star quarterback's rare slip-ups, one of his teammates delivered the play of the day.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tatum scores a season-high 43 to go with 16 rebounds, 10 assist in Celtics 123-98 win over Bulls
item.description: Jayson Tatum scored a season-high 43 points, grabbed 16 rebounds and dished out 10 assists for his third career triple-double and the Boston Celtics beat the Chicago Bulls 123-98 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Flames get goals from 6 players in a 6-4 victory over the Blackhawks
item.description: Jonathan Huberdeau, Connor Zary, Blake Coleman, and Matt Coronato each had a goal and an assist as the Calgary Flames beat the Chicago Blackhawks 6-4 on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jazz win back-to-back games for first time this season
item.description: BROOKLYN, N.Y. (ABC4 Sports) - For the first time since April, the Utah Jazz are on a winning streak. Lauri Markkanen scored 21 points, Collin Sexton and Svi Mykhailiuk each had 18 points, and the Utah Jazz beat the Brooklyn Nets 105-94 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Oakland legend, Hall of Famer Rickey Henderson has died at the age of 65
item.description: (KRON) – Rickey Henderson, one of the greatest baseball players to ever live, died at age 65. Henderson is Major League Baseball’s all-time stolen base leader and is considered by many to be the greatest player in A’s franchise history.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Rickey Henderson, MLB's all-time stolen base leader, dies at 65
item.description: Rickey Henderson, the man who changed Major League Baseball with his blazing, unbeatable speed, has died, the league confirmed. The all-time MLB leader in stolen bases and one of the games greatest leadoff hitters died in Oakland on Friday after a bout with pneumonia, TMZ reports.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5
